hero.bio: Full Stack Developer with experience in frontend and backend development, focused on building scalable applications and adapting quickly to new technologies. Strong problem-solving mindset and ability to work across the full development lifecycle, from concept to deployment.

stack.title: My stack
stack.html5: The standard markup language for structuring web content. Includes native APIs for multimedia, canvas, geolocation, and local storage.
stack.css3: Style sheet language for designing and animating web interfaces. Flexbox, Grid, transitions, and custom properties are its modern pillars.
stack.javascript: The language of the web. Enables browser interactivity and, with Node.js, server-side development too. The foundation of React, Angular, and more.
stack.typescript: A typed superset of JavaScript. Catches errors at compile time and improves autocompletion in large-scale projects.
stack.angular: Google's SPA framework with component-based architecture, dependency injection, and RxJS. Ideal for enterprise-grade applications.
stack.react: Meta's UI library for building reusable components. Virtual DOM, hooks, and a massive ecosystem make it the most popular frontend choice.
stack.reactnative: Build iOS and Android apps with React and JavaScript, compiling to real native components instead of a WebView.
stack.redux: Predictable global state manager for JavaScript. Unidirectional data flow: actions → reducers → store → UI. Widely used alongside React.
stack.nodejs: JavaScript runtime built on Chrome's V8 engine. Ideal for REST APIs, microservices, and real-time applications on the server.
stack.java: Strongly typed, object-oriented, platform-independent language. Widely used in Android development, enterprise applications, and backend systems.
stack.php: Server-side scripting language designed for web development. Powers WordPress, Laravel, and countless CMS systems in production.
stack.laravel: Elegant PHP framework with Eloquent ORM, expressive routing, and Blade templates. A complete ecosystem for modern web applications.
stack.androidstudio: Google's official IDE for Android development. Includes emulators, Layout Editor, and full support for Java and Kotlin.
stack.python: Versatile and readable language, leading in data science, ML, scripting, and automation. An immense scientific library ecosystem.
stack.numpy: The fundamental library for numerical computing in Python. N-dimensional arrays and maths operations optimised in C.
stack.pandas: Data analysis and manipulation library for Python. DataFrames, time series, data cleaning, and aggregation.
stack.matplotlib: Python data visualisation library. Generates static, animated, and interactive charts with full control over styling.
stack.scikitlearn: Python machine learning library with classification, regression, clustering, and dimensionality reduction algorithms ready to use.
stack.tensorflow: Google's deep learning framework. Build and train neural networks for computer vision, NLP, and reinforcement learning.
stack.keras: High-level API built into TensorFlow. Simplifies the definition of layers and deep neural network architectures.
stack.mysql: The most popular open source relational database for the web. Full ACID transaction support and standard SQL.
stack.mariadb: Open source MySQL fork with performance improvements and new storage engines. Fully compatible with the MySQL ecosystem.
stack.git: Distributed version control system. Branches, merges, rebase, and stash to manage the full history of any software project.

projects.title: What I've built
projects.sub: Interactive projects you can explore and try out directly.
projects.bushy_desc: A multiplayer 3D maze game running entirely in the browser. Players explore procedurally generated labyrinths in real time, all connected through a server-managed WebSocket layer.
projects.bushy_h1: Server-side socket management for real-time multiplayer
projects.bushy_h2: 3D world rendering with custom collision detection
projects.bushy_h3: Runs in any browser — no installation needed

experience.title: Experience & Education
experience.sub: A summary of my professional and academic career.
experience.epo_desc: Embedded within the EPO IT department as an external AV technician, providing daily support across 30+ conference and meeting rooms on the Munich campus. Regularly applies full-stack development skills alongside AV expertise to configure, monitor and troubleshoot systems through web-based management platforms.
experience.epo_b1: Daily AV support across 30+ conference rooms for a leading international IP organisation, combining hands-on operation with web-based system management.
experience.epo_b2: Managed 13 Neat video conferencing rooms (Neat Bar, Neat Bar Pro, Neat Center) — installation, firmware updates and remote monitoring via NeatPulse, equivalent to Cisco Webex endpoint management.
experience.epo_b3: Operated Panasonic AV-UHS5M 4K Live Switcher and AW-RP150 PTZ camera controller during live parliamentary sessions and international conferences.
experience.epo_b4: Mixed live audio on Allen & Heath SQ-6 digital mixer: microphone routing, EQ, gain structure and simultaneous translation channel management.
experience.epo_b5: Managed Biamp Tesira DSP audio processing and Televic Plixus conference systems (delegate and chairman units) for multilingual sessions.
experience.epo_b6: Configured and troubleshot Crestron control systems across 20+ rooms via custom touchpanel UIs and Crestron web interfaces.
experience.epo_b7: Applied web development skills daily through browser-based platforms (NeatPulse, Crestron web, Biamp web) for remote diagnostics and system configuration.
experience.epo_b8: Created technical documentation and trained end users on AV equipment operation across the Munich campus.
experience.freelance_desc: Operated independently as a Full Stack Developer, managing the full project lifecycle from requirements gathering to deployment, acquiring clients through Upwork and Fiverr and working remotely with international clients.
experience.freelance_b1: Developed web frontend solutions through platforms such as Upwork and Fiverr.
experience.freelance_b2: Delivered end-to-end application for a client in the defence sector of the UK, built in JavaScript.
experience.freelance_b3: Managed client communication, technical documentation and delivery autonomously using Agile-inspired iterative cycles.
experience.freelance_b4: Maintained top-rated status on both platforms through timely delivery and clear scope management.
experience.freelance_b5: Handled deployment pipelines and hosting configuration for delivered projects.
experience.demesix_desc: DTAXIS is an Android application connecting taxi drivers with passengers in real time. Contributed to the core development team, focusing on user management, location features and device-specific compatibility for the Xiaomi ecosystem.

experience.demesix_b2: Implemented user management, permissions, and location features.
experience.demesix_b3: Integrated MIUI-specific permission handling for Xiaomi devices.

experience.demesix_b6: Participated in daily stand-ups and sprint reviews following Scrum methodology.
experience.galvintec_desc: Part of the development team at a smart building solutions company, building two interconnected products: a web platform for meeting room management and a companion mobile application for on-site use.
experience.galvintec_b1: Developed Angular application for meeting room management and scheduling.
experience.galvintec_b2: Built mobile app using React Native and Cordova.

experience.galvintec_b6: Participated in API design, frontend development, testing and deployment pipeline setup.
experience.hackaboss_desc: Intensive AI & Data programme covering the full spectrum from data analysis to production-ready machine learning models, with hands-on projects applying each concept in real datasets.
